concepts.title: Summation series
concepts.conceptBody:
Sum of first n terms 

Formulae-I
(a) (i) \(\sum_{\mathrm{f}-0}^{\mathrm{n}} \mathrm{C}_{\mathrm{f}}=2^{\mathrm{n}}\)
(ii) \(\sum_{\mathrm{r}-1}^{\mathrm{n}} \mathrm{rC}_{\mathrm{r}}=\mathrm{n}_2{ }^{\mathrm{n}-1}\)
(iii) \(\sum_{\mathrm{r}=1}^{\mathrm{n}} \mathrm{r}^2 \mathrm{C}_{\mathrm{r}}=\mathrm{n}(\mathrm{n}+1) 2^{\mathrm{n}-2}\)
(b) (i) \(\sum_{\mathrm{r}-0}^{\mathrm{n}} \mathrm{C}-0^{\mathrm{s}} \mathrm{C}_{\mathrm{r}}=0\)
(ii) \(\sum_{\mathrm{f}=0}^{\mathrm{n}}(-1)^{\mathrm{r}} \mathrm{C}_{\mathrm{r}}=0\)
(iii) \(\sum_{\mathrm{f}-0}^{\mathrm{n}}(-1)^{\mathrm{r}} \mathrm{r}^2 \mathrm{C}_{\mathrm{r}}=0\)

(Based of formula Set-I) [Remember these formulae carefully] 

Example

Find \(S_n\)
\(\mathrm{S}_{\mathrm{n}}=1 \mathrm{C}_1+3 \mathrm{C}_2+5 \mathrm{C}_3+\ldots(\mathrm{n} \text { terms })\)

Solution : \(\mathrm{T}_{\mathrm{f}}=[1+(\mathrm{r}-1) 2] \mathrm{C}_{\mathrm{r}}=(2 \mathrm{r}-1) \mathrm{C}_{\mathrm{r}}\)

\(\begin{aligned} & \sum_{T=1}^n T_r=\sum_{r=1}^n(2 r-1) C_r \\ & =2 \sum_{r=1}^n \mathrm{rC}_r-\sum_{r=1}^n C_r \\ & =2 n 2^{n-1}-2^n+1 \\ & =n 2^n-2^n+1=2^n(n-1)+1\end{aligned}\)

Proofs of formulae (1) 

\(\sum_{T=0}^{\mathrm{n}}{ }^{\mathrm{n}} \mathrm{C}_{\mathrm{r}}=2^{\mathrm{n}}\)

Proof \((1+x)^n={ }^n C_0+{ }^n C_1 x+{ }^n C_2 x^2+\ldots \ldots+{ }^n C_n x^n\)
\(\begin{gathered}
\text { put } x=1 \\
2^n={ }^n C_0+{ }^n C_1-1 \ldots \ldots . .{ }^n C_n \\
2 \mathrm{n}=\sum_{\mathrm{r}=0} \mathrm{n}^{\mathrm{n}} C_{\mathrm{r}}
\end{gathered}\)
(2) \(\sum_{T=0}^n r^n C_T=n 2^{n-1}\)

Proof L.H.S. \(=r^n C_r\)
\(\begin{aligned}
& =n\left({ }^{n-1} C_{r-1}\right) \\
\sum_{r=0}^n r^n C_r & =n \sum_{r=0}^n{ }^{n+1} C_{r-1} \\
& =n \cdot 2^{n-1}
\end{aligned}\)

Ex. : Find the value of \(\sum_{r=0}^n{ }^{n+2} C_{r+2}\)
Ans. \(\sum_{\mathrm{r}-0}^{\mathrm{n}}{ }^{\mathrm{n}+2} \mathrm{C}_{\mathrm{r}+2}={ }^{\mathrm{n}+2} \mathrm{C}_2+{ }^{\mathrm{n}+2} \mathrm{C}_3 \ldots \ldots .{ }^{\mathrm{n}+2} \mathrm{C}_{\mathrm{r}+2}\)
Always try to expand the series.
Now in this case we will have to add and subtract \({ }^{\mathrm{n}+2} \mathrm{C}_0 \&{ }^{\mathrm{n}+2} \mathrm{C}_1\)
\(\begin{aligned}
& =\left({ }^{n+2} C_0+{ }^{n+2} C_1+\ldots . .{ }^{n+2} C_{n+2}\right)-{ }^{n+2} C_0-{ }^{n+2} C_1 \\
& =\sum_{r=0}^{n+2} C_r^{n+2}-1-(n+2) \\
& =2^{n+2}-1-(n+2)
\end{aligned}\)

Two very important formulas which, we will be using in questions frequently
(1) \((\mathrm{r}+1)^{\mathrm{n}+1} \mathrm{C}_{\mathrm{r}+1}=(\mathrm{n}+1)^{\mathrm{n}} \mathrm{C}_{\mathrm{r}}\)

Proof: \((r+1)^{n+1} C_{r+1}=(r+1) \frac{n+1 !}{r+1 ! n-r !}\)
\(\begin{aligned}
& =(r+1) \times \frac{(n+1) n !}{(r+1) r ! n-r !} \\
& =(n+1) \frac{n !}{r ! n-r !} \\
& (\mathrm{r}+1)^{\mathrm{n}+1} \mathrm{C}_{\mathrm{r}+1}=(\mathrm{n}+1)^{\mathrm{n}} \mathrm{C}_{\mathrm{r}}
\end{aligned}\)
\(\therefore\) We always try to produce this form so that we could convert \((r+1)\) into \((n+1)\).
(2) \(\frac{\mathrm{C}_{\mathrm{r}}}{\mathrm{r}+1}=\frac{1}{(\mathrm{n}+1)}{ }^{\mathrm{n}+1} \mathrm{C}_{\mathrm{r}+1}\)

Proof: RHS \(=\frac{\mathrm{C}_{\mathrm{r}}}{(\mathrm{r}+1)}\)
\(\begin{aligned}
& =\frac{n !}{r ! n-r !(r+1)} \\
& =\frac{(n+1) n !}{(n+1) r+1 ! n-r !} \\
& =\frac{n+1 !}{(r 1+1)(r+1) !(n-r) !} \\
& =\frac{1}{(n+1)}{ }^{n+1} C_{r+1}
\end{aligned}\)
So whenever you get \(r\) term in denominator always try to convert it in given form.
In the same fashion we can write
\(\frac{\mathrm{C}_{\mathrm{r}}}{(\mathrm{r}+1)(\mathrm{r}+1)}=\frac{1}{(\mathrm{n}+1)(\mathrm{n}+1)}{ }^{\mathrm{n}+2} \mathrm{C}_{\mathrm{r}+2}\)

Example
Find \(S_n\) ?
\(\mathrm{S}_{\mathrm{n}}=1^2 \mathrm{C}_0+1^2 \mathrm{C}_1+7^2 \mathrm{C}_2+10^2 \mathrm{C}_3+\ldots(\mathrm{n}+\mathbf{l}) \text { terms }\)

Solution: \((1+(r-1) 3)^2 C_{r+1}=(3 r+1)^2 C_r\)
\(\begin{aligned}
\mathrm{S}_{\mathrm{n}} & =\sum_{\mathrm{r}=0}^{\mathrm{n}} \mathrm{T}_{\mathrm{r}+1}=\sum_{\mathrm{r}=0}^{\mathrm{n}}(3 \mathrm{r}+1)^2 \mathrm{C}_{\mathrm{r}} \\
& =9 \sum_{\mathrm{r}=0}^{\mathrm{n}} \mathrm{r}^2 \mathrm{C}_{\mathrm{r}}+\sum_{\mathrm{r}=0}^{\mathrm{n}} \mathrm{C}_{\mathrm{r}}+6 \sum_{\mathrm{r}=0}^{\mathrm{n}} \mathrm{C}_{\mathrm{r}} \\
& =9 n(n+1) 2^{n-2}+2 n+6^{n-1}
\end{aligned}\)
Example
Find \(\mathrm{S}_{\mathrm{n}}\) ?
\(\left.\mathrm{S}_{\mathrm{n}}=2^2 \mathrm{C}_2+5^2 \mathrm{C}_3+\mathbf{8}^2 \mathrm{C}_4+\ldots \ldots(\mathrm{n}-\mathbf{l}) \text { term } \mathrm{s}\right)\)

Solution: \([2+(\mathrm{r}-1) 3]^2 \mathrm{C}_{\mathrm{r}+1} \Rightarrow \mathrm{T}_{\mathrm{r}-1}=(2+(\mathrm{r}-2) 3)^2 \mathrm{C}_{\mathrm{r}}\)
\(\begin{aligned}
\mathrm{T}_{\mathrm{r}-1} & =(3 \mathrm{r}-4)^2 \mathrm{C}_{\mathrm{r}} \\
\mathrm{Sn} & =\sum_{\mathrm{r}=2}^{\mathrm{n}}(3 \mathrm{r}-4)^2 \mathrm{C}_{\mathrm{r}}=\sum_{\mathrm{r}=1}^{\mathrm{n}}(3 \mathrm{r}-4)^2 \mathrm{C}_{\mathrm{r}}-\mathrm{C}_1 \\
& =9 \sum_{\mathrm{r}=1}^{\mathrm{n}} \mathrm{r}^2 \mathrm{C}_{\mathrm{r}}+16 \sum_{\mathrm{r}=1}^{\mathrm{n}} \mathrm{C}_{\mathrm{r}}-24 \sum_{\mathrm{r}=1}^{\mathrm{n}} \mathrm{r}_{\mathrm{r}}-\mathrm{C}_1 \\
& =9 \mathrm{n}(\mathrm{n}+1) 2^{\mathrm{n}-2}+16\left(2^{\mathrm{n}}-1\right)-24(\mathrm{n}) 2^{\mathrm{n}-1}
\end{aligned}\)

Formulae-II

(i) \(\sum_{\mathrm{f}=0}^{\mathrm{n}} \mathrm{C}_{\mathrm{f}}^2={ }^{2 \mathrm{n}} \mathrm{C}_{\mathrm{n}}\)
(ii) \(\sum_{\uparrow-1}^{\mathrm{n}} \mathrm{rC}_{\mathrm{r}}^2=\mathrm{n}^{2 \mathrm{n}-1} \mathrm{C}_{\mathrm{n}+1}\)
(iii) \(\sum_{\mathrm{r}-1}^{\mathrm{n}} \mathrm{r}^2 \mathrm{C}_{\mathrm{r}}^2=\mathrm{n}^{22 \mathrm{n}-2} \mathrm{C}_{\mathrm{n}-1}\)
(iv) \(\sum\left(\mathrm{C}_0 \mathrm{C}_{\mathrm{r}}+\mathrm{C}_1 \mathrm{C}_{\mathrm{r}+1}+\mathrm{C}_2 \mathrm{C}_{\mathrm{r}+2}\right)\)\(\cdots+C_{n-4} C_n={ }^{2 n} C_{n-5}\)

Remember these formulaes.
You can directly use these formulaes in question.
Questions Based on Formulae Set-II

Example
Find \(S_n\) ?
\(\mathrm{S}_{\mathrm{n}}=\mathrm{C}_1 \mathrm{C}_{\mathrm{r}}+2 \mathrm{C}_2 \mathrm{C}_{\mathrm{r}+1}+3 \mathrm{C}_3 \mathrm{C}_{\mathrm{r}+2}+4 \mathrm{C}_4 \mathrm{C}_{\mathrm{r}+3}+\ldots\)

Solution:
\(\begin{aligned}
& n(1+x)^{n-1}=C_1+2 C_2 x+3 C_3 x^2+4 C_4 x^3+\ldots \\
& (n+1)^n=C_0 x^n+C_1 x^n+C_2 x^{n-1}+\ldots C_r x^{n-r}+C_{r+1} x-n-1 \\
& n(1+x)^{2 n-1} \text { Coeffi. of } x^{n-r} \\
& n\left(2^{n-1} C_{n-7}\right)
\end{aligned}\)

Example
Find \(S_n\) ?
\(1 \mathrm{C}_1{ }^2+3 \mathrm{C}_2{ }^2+5 \mathrm{C}_3{ }^2+\ldots .(\mathrm{n} \text { term } \mathrm{s})\)

Solution:
\(\begin{aligned}
T_r & =[1+(r-1) 2] C_r^2=(2 r-1) C_r^2 \\
S & =\sum T_r=\sum_{r=1}^n(2 r-1) C_r^2=2 \sum_{r=1}^n C_r^2-\sum_{r=1}^n C_r^2 \\
& =2 n^{2 n-1} C_{n-1}-{ }^{2 n} C_n+1
\end{aligned}\)

Example
 Find \(S_n\) ?
\(1^2 \mathrm{C}_0{ }^2+4^2 \mathrm{C}_1{ }^2+7^2 \mathrm{C}_2{ }^2+\ldots(\mathrm{n}+1) \text { term s }\)

Solution:
\(\begin{aligned}
\mathrm{T}_{\mathrm{r}}= & {[1+(\mathrm{r}-1) 3]^2 \mathrm{C}_{\mathrm{r}-1}^2 } \\
\mathrm{~T}_{\mathrm{r}+1} & =(3 \mathrm{r}+1)^2 \mathrm{C}_{\mathrm{r}}^2 \\
\mathrm{~S}_{\mathrm{n}} & =\sum_{\mathrm{r}=0}^{\mathrm{n}}\left(9 \mathrm{r}^2+1+6 \mathrm{r}\right) \mathrm{C}_{\mathrm{r}}^2 \\
& =9 \sum_{\mathrm{r}=0}^{\mathrm{n}} \mathrm{r}^2 \mathrm{C}_{\mathrm{r}}^2+\sum_{\mathrm{r}=0}^{\mathrm{n}} \mathrm{C}_{\mathrm{r}}^2+6 \sum_{\mathrm{r}=0}^{\mathrm{n}} \mathrm{r} \mathrm{C}_{\mathrm{r}} \\
& =9 \mathrm{n}^{22 n-2} \mathrm{C}_{\mathrm{n}-1}+{ }^{2 n} \mathrm{C}_{\mathrm{n}}+6 \mathrm{n} 2^{\mathrm{n}-1} \mathrm{C}_{\mathrm{n}-1}
\end{aligned}\)

Example
Find \(\mathrm{S}_{\mathrm{n}}\) ?
\(2^2 \mathrm{C}_2{ }^2+5^2 \mathrm{C}_3{ }^2+\mathbf{8}^2 \mathrm{C}_4{ }^2+\ldots(\mathrm{n}+\mathbf{1}) \text { terms }\)

Solution:
\(\begin{aligned}
\mathrm{T}_{\mathrm{r}} & =[2+(\mathrm{r}-1) 3]^2 \mathrm{C}_{\mathrm{r}+1}^2 \\
\mathrm{~T}_{\mathrm{r}-1} & =(2+3 \mathrm{r}-6)^2 \mathrm{C}_{\mathrm{r}}^2=(3 \mathrm{r}-4)^2 \mathrm{C}_{\mathrm{r}}^2 \\
\mathrm{~S}_{\mathrm{n}} & =\sum_{\mathrm{r}-2}^{\mathrm{n}}(3 \mathrm{r}-4)^2 \mathrm{C}_{\mathrm{r}}^2 \\
\mathrm{~S}_{\mathrm{n}} & =\sum_{\mathrm{r}=1}^{\mathrm{n}}(3 \mathrm{r}-4)^2 \mathrm{C}_{\mathrm{r}}^2-\mathrm{n}^2 \\
& =9 \sum_{\mathrm{r}-1}^{\mathrm{n}} \mathrm{r}^2 \mathrm{C}_{\mathrm{r}}^2+16 \sum_{\mathrm{r}-1}^{\mathrm{n}} \mathrm{C}_{\mathrm{r}}^2-24 \sum_{\mathrm{r}-1}^{\mathrm{n}} \mathrm{r}_{\mathrm{r}}^2-\mathrm{n}^2
\end{aligned}\)

Example
Find \(S_n\) ?
\(\frac{\mathbf{C}_0}{\mathbf{1}}+\frac{\mathrm{C}_1}{2}+\frac{\mathrm{C}_2}{3}+\ldots+\frac{\mathrm{C}_{\mathrm{n}}}{\mathbf{n}}\)

Solution:
(Here in this series there are total \((n+1)\) forms).
In \(T_{r+1}\) th term if we vary value of \(r\) from 0 to \(n\) we will get summation of all terms.
\(\begin{aligned}
\mathrm{T}_{\mathrm{r}} & =\frac{\mathrm{C}_{\mathrm{r}-1}}{\mathrm{r}}=\mathrm{T}_{\mathrm{r}+1}=\frac{\mathrm{C}_{\mathrm{r}}}{\mathrm{r}+1} \\
\mathrm{~S}_{\mathrm{n}} & =\sum_{\mathrm{r}-0}^{\mathrm{n}} \frac{\mathrm{C}_{\mathrm{r}}}{\mathrm{r}+1}=\frac{1}{\mathrm{n}+1} \sum_{\mathrm{r}-0}^{\mathrm{n}+1} \mathrm{C}_{\mathrm{r}+1} \\
& =\left(\frac{2^{n+1}-1}{n+1}\right)
\end{aligned}\)

Example
Find \(S_n\) ?
\(\frac{\mathrm{C}_0}{1.2}+\frac{\mathrm{C}_1}{2.3}+\frac{\mathrm{C}_2}{3.4}\)

Solution:
\(\left.\begin{array}{l}
\mathrm{T}_{\mathrm{r}}=\frac{\mathrm{C}_{\mathrm{r}-1}}{\mathrm{r}(\mathrm{r}+1)} \Rightarrow \mathrm{T}_{\mathrm{r}+1}=\frac{\mathrm{C}_{\mathrm{r}}}{(\mathrm{r}+1)(\mathrm{r}+2)} \\
\mathrm{S}_{\mathrm{n}}=\sum_{\mathrm{r}=0}^{\mathrm{n}} \frac{\mathrm{C}_{\mathrm{r}}}{(\mathrm{r}+1)(\mathrm{r}+2)}=\frac{1}{(\mathrm{n}+1)(\mathrm{n}+2)} \sum_{\mathrm{r}=0}^{\mathrm{n}}{ }^{\mathrm{n}+2} \mathrm{C}_{\mathrm{r}+2} \\
=\frac{2^{\mathrm{n}+2}-1-(\mathrm{n}+2)}{(\mathrm{n}+1)(\mathrm{n}+2)}
\end{array}\right\} \text { (here we apply the formula (2) }\)

Example
Find \(S_n\) ?
\(\mathrm{S}_{\mathrm{n}}=\frac{\mathrm{C}_0}{\mathbf{2}}+\frac{\mathrm{C}_1}{\mathbf{3}}+\frac{\mathrm{C}_2}{\mathbf{4}}+\ldots\)

Solution:
\(\mathrm{T}_{\mathrm{r}}=\frac{\mathrm{C}_{\mathrm{r}-1}}{\mathrm{r}+1}=\mathrm{T}_{\mathrm{r}+1} \Rightarrow \frac{\mathrm{C}_{\mathrm{r}}}{(\mathrm{r}+2)}\)
here we multiplied \((r+1)\) both in denominator and nominator to convert it into formula 2.
\(\begin{aligned}
& =\frac{1}{(n+1)(n+2)} \sum_{r=0}^n(r+2-1)^{n+2} C_{r+2} \\
& =\frac{1}{(n+1)(n+2)}\left[\sum_{r=0}^n(r+2)^{n+2} C_{r+2}-\sum_{r=0}^n{ }^{n+2} C_{r+2}\right] \\
& =\frac{1}{(n+1)(n+2)}\left[(n+2) 2^{n+1}-(n+2)-\left\{2^{n+2}-1-(n+2)\right\}\right] \\
& =\frac{n 2^{n+1}+1}{(n+1)(n+2)}
\end{aligned}\)

 

IMP. RESULTS

(1) The sum of binomial coefficient in \((1+x)^n\) is \(2^n\).

Proof \((1+x)^n=C_0+C_1 x+C_2 x^2+\ldots \ldots+C_n x^n\)
Putting \(\mathrm{x}=1\)
\(2^{\mathrm{n}}=\mathrm{C}_0+\mathrm{C}_1+\mathrm{C}_2+\ldots \ldots \ldots \ldots+\mathrm{C}_{\mathrm{n}}\)

Example
Prove that the sum of the coefficients in the expression \(\left(1+x-3 x^2\right)^{2163}\) is ' -1 '.
Solution:
Putting \(x=1\) in \(\left(1+x-3 x^2\right)^{2163}\)
Sum of the coefficients
\(\begin{aligned}
& =(1+1-3)^{2163} \\
& =(-1)^{2163}=-1
\end{aligned}\)
(2) The sum of the coefficients of the odd-terms in \((1+x)^n\) is equal to the sum of coefficients of the even terms and each is equal to \(2^{\mathrm{n}-1}\).
Proof: Putting \(x=-1\), in eg. (1):
\(\mathrm{O}=\mathrm{C}_0-\mathrm{C}_1+\mathrm{C}_2-\mathrm{C}_3+\ldots \ldots \ldots+(-1)^{\mathrm{n}} \mathrm{C}_{\mathrm{n}}\)
and from (ii): \(2^{\mathrm{n}}=\mathrm{C}_0+\mathrm{C}_1+\mathrm{C}_2+\ldots \ldots \ldots . .+\mathrm{C}_{\mathrm{n}}\)
Adding these \(\mathrm{eg}^{\mathrm{n}}\) :
\(2^{\mathrm{n}}=2\left(\mathrm{C}_0+\mathrm{C}_2+\mathrm{C}_4+\ldots \ldots \ldots\right)\)
or \(\mathrm{C}_0+\mathrm{C}_2+\mathrm{C}_4+=2^{\mathrm{n}-1}\)
Subtracting these \(\mathrm{eg}^{\mathrm{n}}\) :
\(2^{\mathrm{n}}=2\left(\mathrm{C}_1+\mathrm{C}_3+\mathrm{C}_5+\ldots \ldots \ldots \ldots \ldots\right)\)

Or \(\mathrm{C}_1+\mathrm{C}_3+\mathrm{C}_5+\ldots \ldots \ldots \ldots \ldots=2^{\mathrm{n}-1}\)
From (iii) and (iv):
\(\mathrm{C}_0+\mathrm{C}_2+\mathrm{C}_4+\ldots \ldots \ldots \ldots \ldots=\mathrm{C}_1+\mathrm{C}_3+\mathrm{C}_5+\ldots \ldots \ldots \ldots \ldots 2^{\mathrm{n}-1}\)

If $(1+x)^n=C_0+C_1 x+C_2 x^2+\ldots+C_n x^n$, then the value of $C_0+C_2+C_4+C_6+\ldots .$. is
The value of $\frac{C_1}{2}+\frac{C_3}{4}+\frac{C_5}{6}+\ldots .$. is equal to
The sum to $(n+1)$ terms of the following series $\frac{C_0}{2}-\frac{C_1}{3}+\frac{C_2}{4}-\frac{C_3}{5}+$ is
